controls.title: Principles relating to processing of personal data
controls.summary: Personal data shall be processed lawfully, fairly, transparently; collected for specified purposes; adequate, relevant, limited; accurate; stored no longer than necessary; and processed with appropriate security.

controls.title: Lawfulness of processing
controls.summary: Processing is lawful only if at least one of: consent, contractual necessity, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, or legitimate interests applies.

controls.title: Conditions for consent
controls.summary: Consent must be freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous. Must be as easy to withdraw consent as to give it. Burden of proof on the controller.

controls.title: Processing of special categories of personal data
controls.summary: Prohibition on processing special category data (health, biometric, racial, political, religious, union membership, genetic, sexual orientation) unless an explicit exception applies.

controls.title: Transparent information, communication and modalities
controls.summary: Controller must provide information to data subjects in a concise, transparent, intelligible, and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language.

controls.title: Information to be provided at collection
controls.summary: At time of collection from the data subject, the controller must provide identity, contact, purposes, legal basis, recipients, retention, and subject rights information.

controls.title: Information from third-party sources
controls.summary: Where personal data was not collected directly, the controller must still provide Art. 13-equivalent information within one month of obtaining the data.

controls.title: Right of access by the data subject
controls.summary: Data subjects have the right to obtain confirmation of processing, a copy of their personal data, and supplementary information within one month.

controls.title: Right to rectification
controls.summary: Data subjects have the right to obtain rectification of inaccurate personal data and completion of incomplete personal data without undue delay.

controls.title: Right to erasure ('right to be forgotten')
controls.summary: Data subjects may request erasure when data is no longer necessary, consent is withdrawn, an objection prevails, or processing is unlawful.

controls.title: Right to restriction of processing
controls.summary: Data subjects may restrict processing during the period of contesting accuracy, pending objection assessment, or when processing is unlawful but the subject opposes erasure.

controls.title: Right to data portability
controls.summary: Data subjects have the right to receive their data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format and to transmit it to another controller, where technically feasible.

controls.title: Right to object
controls.summary: Data subjects may object to processing based on legitimate interests, direct marketing, or scientific/historical research. Controller must stop unless compelling legitimate grounds override.

controls.title: Automated decision-making including profiling
controls.summary: Data subjects have the right not to be subject to solely automated decisions with significant effects, including profiling, unless consent, contractual necessity, or EU law applies.

controls.title: Data protection by design and by default
controls.summary: Controllers must implement data protection principles into processing design and ensure that by default only data necessary for each purpose is processed.

controls.title: Processor obligations and DPA
controls.summary: Processing by a processor must be governed by a binding Data Processing Agreement (DPA). Processor acts only on documented instructions of the controller.

controls.title: Records of processing activities (RoPA)
controls.summary: Controllers and processors must maintain a written record of all processing activities under their responsibility, available to the supervisory authority on request.

controls.title: Security of processing
controls.summary: Implement appropriate technical and organisational measures including pseudonymisation, encryption, confidentiality, integrity, availability, resilience, and regular testing.

controls.title: Notification of breach to supervisory authority
controls.summary: Personal data breaches must be notified to the competent supervisory authority within 72 hours of the controller becoming aware, where feasible.

controls.title: Communication of breach to the data subject
controls.summary: When a breach is likely to result in a high risk to natural persons, the controller must communicate the breach to the data subject without undue delay.

controls.title: Data protection impact assessment (DPIA)
controls.summary: A DPIA is required prior to processing where processing is likely to result in a high risk, in particular using new technologies, systematic profiling, or large-scale sensitive data.

controls.title: Designation of the data protection officer
controls.summary: Mandatory DPO required for public authorities, organisations with large-scale systematic monitoring, or large-scale special category processing.

controls.title: General principle for transfers
controls.summary: Personal data may only be transferred to third countries where an adequate level of protection is ensured (adequacy decision, SCCs, BCRs, or derogations).

controls.title: Transfers subject to appropriate safeguards
controls.summary: Transfers may occur if the controller or processor provides appropriate safeguards (SCCs, BCRs, codes of conduct, certification, etc.) and enforceable rights are available.
